a_image.alt: Skeuomorphism style mood board — Design Philosophy · 2007–2013

sections.a_description: Digital interfaces that look and feel like physical objects — leather-bound notebooks, brushed aluminum panels, stitched edges, and buttons so glossy they practically invite you to press them. Skeuomorphism is the design philosophy that defined the iPhone's first era, where a Notes app looked like a yellow legal pad and a bookshelf app displayed actual wooden shelves.

Skeuomorphic design appears on retro iOS appreciation sites, premium app landing pages, and creative portfolios that value tactile richness. You'll find it on music production tool interfaces, luxury brand microsites, and design retrospective projects — anywhere that believes digital interfaces should have the warmth and dimensionality of real-world objects.

Rich, tactile interface design where digital elements mimic their real-world counterparts — leather textures, brushed metal, stitched borders, glossy buttons that look pressable. Background: a subtle linen texture (#e8e0d0) created via a fine repeating-linear-gradient pattern. The .inner panel is a leather-bound card — warm brown (#5d4037) with a subtle inner border using a 2px inset lighter brown (#8d6e63), stitched effect via a 1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.3) border offset inward, rounded corners (10px). Text is warm cream (#faf0e6) on the dark leather. A glossy sheen pseudo-element: a diagonal white-to-transparent gradient at 10% opacity across the top of the card. Buttons are glossy capsules with a vertical gradient and a white highlight band. Set in Inter — a clean, neutral sans-serif that lets the textural design elements speak without competing typographically, channeling the Helvetica Neue sensibility of iOS 6. Heading at weight 700, 30px, letter-spacing -0.5px, line-height 1.2, cream (#faf0e6) with a subtle dark text-shadow for depth. Eyebrow at weight 500, 12px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color rgba(250,240,230,0.7). Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.7, color rgba(250,240,230,0.85). Primary button: glossy green gradient (#7cb342 to #558b2f) with white text, inset white highlight, rounded (8px). Secondary: semi-transparent (#ffffff20) with cream text. Alternatives: Nunito (rounder), SF Pro Display (Apple native), Roboto (Android material).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
